task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS Administration dashboard or login screen is visible in a web browser.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Add Concept Source page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Concept Source' or 'Manage Concept Sources' page is open in the OpenMRS interface.
The text 'ICD-11' is entered into the Name input field.
The text 'International Classification of Diseases 11th Revision - WHO standard for coding diseases disorders injuries and other health conditions' is entered into the Description input field.
The text 'I11' is entered into the HL7 Code input field.
A success message (e.g., 'Concept Source saved') is displayed, or 'ICD-11' is visible as a saved entry in the list of concept sources.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser to interact with OpenMRS
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction within a web browser navigating to https://localhost/openmrs/admin, rather than using terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal API calls to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using 'curl' or python scripts in a terminal to send POST requests to the /openmrs/ws/rest/v1/conceptsource API endpoint.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots display the standard OpenMRS web interface styling and layout, not a text editor or a locally mocked HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Name: 'ICD-11'. Expected Description: 'International Classification of Diseases 11th Revision - WHO standard for coding diseases disorders injuries and other health conditions' (accept with or without commas). Expected HL7 Code: 'I11'. The task must be completed via the OpenMRS Administration web interface, not via direct API calls.
